item.title: Hybrid Bond Sales Reach Record as Companies Pounce on Low Costs
item.description: Companies are selling hybrid bonds at a record pace, seeking to pad their balance sheets while the extra cost of the risky debt hovers near an all-time low.

item.title: Peloton Raises Full-Year Outlook, Signaling Turnaround on Track
item.description: Peloton Interactive Inc. raised its guidance for the full year, suggesting that a turnaround fueled by new commercial offerings and upgraded equipment is on track.

item.title: ‘Teflon Musk’ Rises Again in Twitter Deal With SEC
item.description: Billionaire pays $1.5 million in settlement over disclosures of Twitter stock purchases

item.title: Europe Braces for a Fresh Cycle of Chinese Competition
item.description: For Europe, Beijing’s latest 5-year plan is the latest articulation of threats to its prosperity.

item.title: DP World Introduces War Risk Insurance for Mideast Cargo
item.description: Billed as end-to-end coverage, it protects “critical gaps” in global supply chains, DP World says

item.title: Credit Karma Opens Site for Americans With No Credit History
item.description: Intuit Credit Karma is opening its site to Americans who have no credit history, allowing them to take the first steps to building credit.

item.title: Google Translate Rival DeepL Announces Plans to Cut 25% of Staff
item.description: DeepL, the German startup building translation tools, announced plans to cut approximately 25% of its workforce, a move its Chief Executive Officer Jarek Kutylowski attributed to the “massive structural shift” from artificial intelligence.

item.title: BCE Beats on AI-Powered Business, Higher Revenue
item.description: BCE Inc. beat analyst expectations in the first quarter as its investments in artificial intelligence infrastructure pay off.

item.title: Two Reasons to Pay Close Attention to London Tonight
item.description: At least one of them may have global implications.

item.title: Fed’s Collins Favored Changing FOMC Statement With Dissenters
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston President Susan Collins said she agreed with colleagues who dissented at last week’s monetary policy gathering over wording in the Fed’s post-meeting statement that suggested the central bank would eventually resume rate reductions.

item.title: Krispy Kreme Posts Mixed Earnings, Sharpens Debt-Reduction Goal
item.description: Krispy Kreme Inc. reported earnings that were broadly in line with estimates and tightened its debt reduction target, signaling its recovery plan is gaining traction.

item.title: Trump’s Affordability Push Stalls Ahead of Midterm Elections
item.description: The Trump administration’s fitful campaign to address US voters’ affordability concerns ahead of the midterm elections is faltering, with some key efforts stalled, cast aside or limited in scope.

item.title: Wall Street Debates Whether 5% Yields Are Here to Stay
item.description: Wall Street is debating whether 30-year Treasury yields will mount a sustained push over the key level.

item.title: Foundering: Bob Lee, the Tech Industry, and the City That’s Become Its Reluctant Home
item.description: A year before Bob Lee was killed, San Francisco recalled its progressive district attorney, Chesa Boudin, in a campaign heavily funded by venture capitalists. They rallied around fear of crime.

item.title: CoreWeave’s Stunning Rally Creates Prove-It Moment for Earnings
item.description: CoreWeave Inc. shares are on a scorching run in 2026 as demand for computing capacity to power artificial intelligence keeps growing. But now investors want to see some proof that the neo-cloud provider is executing on its ambitious plans.

item.title: China’s Leapmotor Expands in Germany With EV Costing €49 a Month
item.description: China’s Leapmotor is targeting a bigger slice of Europe’s largest auto market with an electric vehicle costing less than a typical monthly mobile-phone bill.

item.title: Wall Street Firms Post Dozens of Crypto Jobs — With a Catch
item.description: Having a splash of Wall Street on a crypto résumé has become a way to stay gainfully employed.

item.title: Carlyle Earnings Tumble 28% as Carried Interest Remains Elusive
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc.’s first-quarter earnings tumbled as robust asset sales from its most recent buyout funds have yet to generate carried interest.

item.title: Oil Loading Logjam at Crucial Oman Port Puts Flows Under Strain
item.description: Delays to crude loading at a vital Omani terminal outside of the Strait of Hormuz last month has upended collection schedules and may lead to late deliveries to buyers already starved of Middle Eastern supply.

item.title: US Yields at 5% Tug Traders Between Dip-Buying Greed and Fear
item.description: In some circles on Wall Street, the hottest debate isn’t about a tech-stock bubble or $100 oil as the new norm. It’s whether 30-year Treasury yields will mount a sustained push over 5%.

item.title: US Tech-Sector Layoffs Keep Mounting as Other Job Cuts Decline
item.description: Planned job cuts continued to mount in the US technology sector last month, bringing the year-to-date total to a three-year high even as overall private-sector layoff announcements receded.

item.title: Cambodia Signals Possible Asean Reopening for Myanmar Leaders
item.description: Cambodia signaled a possible opening to ease Myanmar’s isolation within the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, as sentiment by some members is warming toward bringing the crisis-worn nation’s leadership back into the fold.

item.title: Kalshi Does No Better Than Experts on Key Jobs Forecasting Test
item.description: Prediction markets have promised traders more accurate, real-time forecasts on crucial economic data releases.

item.title: Japan Likely Used $30 Billion in Follow-Up Yen Intervention
item.description: Japanese authorities likely used around $30 billion intervening in the currency market just days after an earlier round of action, according to a Bloomberg analysis of central bank accounts, in the latest indication of their resolve to support the yen.

item.title: Business Owners Everywhere Can’t Escape Iran War’s Ripple Effects
item.description: From lost income to worsening inflation, small and midsize operators around the globe are feeling the pain.

item.title: Banxico Set to Deliver Final Cut in Easing Cycle After Economy Shrank
item.description: Mexican policymakers will likely end their two-year easing cycle with a final interest rate cut on Thursday after the central bank chief signaled her willingness for one more drop and the economy contracted.

item.title: Kraft Heinz Joins Reverse Yankee Boom to Fund Debt Buyback
item.description: Kraft Heinz Foods Co is hitting Europe’s bond market for the first time in over a year to raise euro-currency debt that will help fund a buyback of dollar notes.

item.title: Two Indian Conglomerates to Plow $1 Billion in EV, Battery Tech
item.description: Two Indian conglomerates are looking to spend nearly $1 billion to build domestic capabilities in electric-vehicle and battery technologies, people familiar with the matter said, reflecting a growing urgency on the part of the country’s biggest companies to reduce their reliance on Chinese technology.

item.title: UK Construction Slump Gets Worse as Cost Pressures Intensify
item.description: British builders posted the largest decline in output since November as the Iran war pushed up input prices and discouraged sales.

item.title: US, Iran Deliberate Peace Deal With Nuclear Breakthrough Distant
item.description: The US is waiting on Iran to respond to its proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end a war that’s killed thousands of people and triggered a surge in energy prices.
